mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE is below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and construction investment, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS growth is positive but HOUST declines, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and business activity, which translates to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during periods of rising inflation expectations, or the signal reverses direction when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds a 2.5% annualized threshold, markets anticipate aggressive monetary tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 70% of months where CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 2.5%, or the threshold effect disappears when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer confidence and equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day returns when PAYEMS growth decelerates while UNRATE remains flat, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases aggregate corporate revenues and profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC consistently falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a sudden contraction in manufacturing output and supply chain disruptions, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO drops > 0.5%, or the signal fails to predict returns when RSAFS remains stable.
